verdict_reasoning: TMED10 (TMP21/p23) is a type I single-pass transmembrane protein with its dominant steady-state localization in the ER, ERGIC, and Golgi cisternae, where it functions as a cargo receptor in COPI/COPII vesicular trafficking. However, multiple independent lines of evidence document a minority plasma membrane pool: (1) the NCBI/RefSeq annotation explicitly states it 'is localized to the plasma membrane and golgi cisternae'; (2) published work confirms TMED10 localizes to the plasma membrane independently of COPI; (3) a 2019 study documents that TMED10 cycles through the plasma membrane with misfolded GPI-anchored proteins; (4) RAB21 studies discuss RAB21-mediated recycling of TMED10 from the PM back to the Golgi, consistent with a constitutive cycling pool. The protein has its own single-pass TM domain and an extracellular GOLD-domain lumenal face, so when present at the PM its ectodomain is accessible from outside the cell. This fits dual_localization: dominant endomembrane compartment with a documented minority PM cycling pool. Pre-no checklist: cell_state_induced — not the primary mechanism but cycling is constitutive; tissue_restricted — PM presence is not tissue-restricted; lysosomal_exocytosis — not lysosomal; dual_localization — YES, documented; stable_surface_attachment — not applicable.
